meta.title: form-human-guard — a lightweight pre-submit direction matching guard for forms
meta.description: A lightweight pre-submit direction matching guard for forms. Not a CAPTCHA replacement — an honest, zero-dependency interaction guard with one-time HMAC tokens, server-side timing, and explainable risk scoring.

hero.tagline: A lightweight pre-submit direction matching guard for forms.
hero.sub: Show an arrow, ask for the gesture, verify on the server, issue a one-time token. A small, honest layer that filters out low-effort bots — without CAPTCHAs, cookies, or fingerprinting.

honesty.heading: What this is — and isn't
honesty.statement: This package is not a full CAPTCHA replacement. The expected direction is visible to the client, so a targeted bot can always pass it. It is a lightweight pre-submit interaction guard designed to reduce low-effort automated form abuse. Use it together with rate limiting, server-side validation, and normal authentication security controls.
honesty.is1: A pre-submit human-interaction guard that filters out low-effort bots and accidental submits
honesty.is2: A short-lived, one-time token flow with server-side timing checks
honesty.is3: Deterministic, explainable risk scoring — no ML, no fingerprinting, no coordinates

demoDirection.heading: DirectionGuard — the visible widget
demoDirection.lead: This is the real component talking to this site's real API. Perform the shown direction by dragging or with your arrow keys, and watch every request and response in the X-ray panel.
demoDirection.difficultyNote: Difficulty is server-authoritative: the selector here is honoured only because this demo endpoint explicitly opts in — a client can never downgrade your production settings.
demoDirection.xrayEmpty: Interact with the widget and the guard's network traffic will appear here.
demoDirection.tokenNote: This token is bound to the "demo" action and dies on first use. The newsletter form below would reject it with 403 ACTION_MISMATCH.

demoSubmit.heading: SubmitGuard — invisible until submit
demoSubmit.lead: Nothing is rendered in the form but a hidden input. Press “Create account” and the submit is intercepted: rotate the arrow to match the target, and the form continues with a fresh one-time token.
demoSubmit.shadowNote: The overlay renders in a closed ShadowRoot: this page's CSS can't restyle it and scripts can't reach into it. Theming works through the preset + override tokens you're using right here.
demoSubmit.fakeNote: A fake form — nothing is stored. The guard and the token verification are real.
demoSubmit.interceptNote: This form is protected by form-human-guard — the button above will be intercepted.
demoSubmit.reuseExplain: The token was deleted server-side on first use. Every replay fails with TOKEN_NOT_FOUND — that's the one-time guarantee.

how.heading: Five steps, one honest contract
how.lead: The client only ever proves “a human-shaped interaction happened just now”. Everything that matters — timing, verdicts, tokens — lives on the server.

challenge.title: Challenge
challenge.desc: The client fetches a randomized challenge; the server stores it with a 120 s TTL.

interact.title: Interact
interact.desc: The user drags, taps, or uses arrow keys. Fully keyboard-operable.

verify.title: Server verify
verify.desc: Direction, attempt limits, and elapsed time — all measured server-side.

token.title: One-time token
token.desc: An HMAC-signed token bound to your action, valid for 180 s, single use.

consume.title: Consume
consume.desc: Your protected endpoint verifies and deletes the token. Replays fail.

timing.title: Server-side timing
timing.desc: Solve time is computed from the server's own clock — the client-reported duration is telemetry only and can only ever raise suspicion, never lower it.

binding.title: Action binding
binding.desc: Tokens are scoped to an action. A token earned on the demo can't be spent on the newsletter — the mismatch fails with 403 ACTION_MISMATCH.

oneTime.title: One-time tokens
oneTime.desc: Verification deletes the token server-side. The same token presented twice fails with 401 — try it yourself in the demo above.

risk.heading: Explainable rules, not a black box
risk.lead: Direction-match verifications pass through deterministic, rule-based scoring. These three scenarios run through the actual engine — same rules, same thresholds as production.

human.label: Human-like
human.desc: A normal solve: ~3 seconds, a few rotation steps, plausible event counts. No rule fires.

signalless.label: Signal-less
signalless.desc: The correct answer arrives, but with no interaction signals at all — like a script replaying the HTTP flow without a UI.

impossible.label: Impossible rotation
impossible.desc: Solved near the minimum time, reporting fewer rotation steps than the target physically requires.

risk.noReasons: No rules fired — the interaction looks human.
risk.note: No ML, no fingerprinting, no coordinates — the client reports three counters, the server validates them, and every decision carries its reasons. Reasons stay on the server: they'd be a cheat sheet for attackers.

code.heading: Wire it up in a few lines
code.lead: React on the front, any Node server on the back. The server modules import neither React nor Express.
code.nextNote: This is the exact pattern this site runs on — Route Handlers plus Upstash Redis.

teaser.heading: The core stays MIT. Forever.
teaser.lead: Everything you've tried on this page is and stays free. Exclusive is a planned commercial tier for teams running the guard in production.
teaser.promise: No prices, no checkout, no fake screenshots — it doesn't exist yet, and we won't pretend otherwise.

free.name: Free (OSS)
free.blurb: The full package: both guard modes, tokens, risk scoring, themes, stores. MIT-licensed.

pro.name: Pro
pro.blurb: Hosted verification API, analytics dashboard, exclusive challenge modes, rule packs.

enterprise.name: Enterprise
enterprise.blurb: SLA, self-hosted licensing of exclusive modes, SSO, audit logs, custom rules.

newsletter.heading: Get release notes
newsletter.lead: New modes, new rules, breaking changes — a short email when something ships. Nothing else.
newsletter.consentLabel: I agree to receive release-note emails. Unsubscribe anytime with one click — we store your address and nothing else.
newsletter.successNote: You just passed a real SubmitGuard challenge to get here.
newsletter.protectedNote: This form is protected by form-human-guard with action "newsletter" — try submitting it. A token from the demo above would be rejected here.
newsletter.genericError: Something went wrong. Please try again.
newsletter.invalidEmail: That doesn't look like a valid email address.
newsletter.rateLimited: Too many requests — please wait a minute and try again.

footer.disclaimer: Not a CAPTCHA replacement: a targeted bot can always pass a client-visible challenge. This is a lightweight guard against low-effort form abuse — keep your rate limiting and validation.

cookie.message: We currently use only strictly necessary cookies: your consent choice, language, and theme preferences. No analytics, no tracking pixels.

docs.englishOnly: Documentation is currently English-only. Translations are planned.

meta.title: Exclusive — planned commercial tier
meta.description: The core stays MIT, forever. Exclusive is a planned commercial tier: hosted verification, analytics, exclusive challenge modes. Join the waitlist.

exclusive.heading: The core is MIT-licensed, forever. Exclusive adds what teams need in production.
exclusive.lead: Open core, honestly: everything the package does today stays free. Exclusive is the planned layer on top — for teams who'd rather not run the infrastructure themselves.
exclusive.openCorePromise: The open-core promise: no existing feature will ever move behind a paywall. If it's in the MIT package today, it stays there.
exclusive.noPriceNote: No pricing yet — nothing here is for sale. Waitlist members get early-bird pricing when it ships.
exclusive.freeNote: Install it now: npm install form-human-guard
exclusive.honestyNote: The same honesty applies here: a hosted verification API does not magically stop targeted bots either. Exclusive is about operating the guard comfortably at scale — dashboards, insight, support — not about stronger magic.

free.name: Free (OSS)
free.blurb: Everything the package does today. This card exists to make the promise concrete.
free.f1: Both guard modes: DirectionGuard and SubmitGuard
free.f2: One-time HMAC tokens, action binding, server-side timing
free.f3: Deterministic risk scoring with custom rules
free.f4: All 6 overlay themes + full override model
free.f5: MemoryStore and RedisStore, Express adapter, framework-agnostic server utils

pro.name: Pro (planned)
pro.blurb: Production operations without running the infrastructure.
pro.f1: Hosted verification API — a drop-in endpoint, no Redis to run
pro.f3: Risk-decision insights: which rules fire, where, how often
pro.f4: Additional exclusive challenge modes and configurable rule packs

enterprise.name: Enterprise (planned)
enterprise.blurb: For teams with compliance and scale requirements.

waitlist.heading: Join the waitlist
waitlist.lead: Help us gauge demand — and get early-bird pricing when Exclusive ships. Protected by the guard itself, naturally.
waitlist.noteLabel: What would make this a must-buy?
waitlist.notePlaceholder: e.g. a dashboard my whole team can read, or SOC 2 paperwork…
waitlist.successNote: We'll email you when there's something real to show — and you just solved a real waitlist-bound challenge to get here.
waitlist.protectedNote: This form is protected by form-human-guard with action "waitlist" — dogfooding on the page that matters most.
waitlist.genericError: Something went wrong. Please try again.
waitlist.invalidEmail: That doesn't look like a valid email address.
waitlist.rateLimited: Too many requests — please wait a minute and try again.

free.a: Yes. The package is MIT-licensed and stays that way, forever. No existing feature will ever move behind a paywall — Exclusive is additive (hosted infrastructure, dashboards, extra modes), not subtractive.

price.a: Undecided — we won't invent a number to look established. Waitlist members get early-bird pricing when it ships.

when.a: No date promised. The waitlist exists precisely to gauge whether and what to build first.

selfhost.q: Can I self-host the Exclusive features?
selfhost.a: Self-hosted licensing of the exclusive challenge modes is planned for the Enterprise tier. The open-source core is self-hosted by definition.

captcha.a: No — and Exclusive won't turn it into one. The expected direction is visible to the client, so a targeted bot can always pass; that stays true no matter who hosts the verification. See the honesty section on the landing page.
